channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Vikram Misri to visit Nepal for high-level bilateral talks with new government
item.description: Vikram Misri, India's Foreign Secretary, is scheduled to visit Nepal next month in what will be the first high-level bilateral engagement between India and Nepal's new government led by Prime Minister...

item.title: India Among Top Five Military Spenders Globally in 2025, Pakistan at 31st Position
item.description: According to the latest report from the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (Sipri), India was the fifth-largest military spender in the world during 2025, following the United States, Ch...

item.title: Elon Musk and OpenAI Clash in Court Over Alleged Broken Promises
item.description: For San Francisco, jury selection don start for courtroom wahala wey dey involve Elon Musk and OpenAI. Musk dey accuse the startup wey e once support say dem don waka away from their non-profit goal o...

item.title: UAT highlights ongoing struggles with poverty, land access and unemployment on Freedom Day
item.description: As South Africa observes Freedom Day, the United Africans Transformation (UAT) party has highlighted that true freedom is still a distant reality for many citizens. While the nation celebrates the end...

item.title: Quebec Premier Begins First International Trip to Strengthen Trade and Diplomatic Ties
item.description: Quebec Premier has undertaken her first international trip since assuming office, visiting Washington D.C. to meet with U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer. The visit aims to strengthen trade rel...

item.title: Philippines Submits Documents to Interpol for Red Notice Against Zaldy Co
item.description: Ayon sa Philippine Center on Transnational Crime (PCTC), naipadala na nila sa Interpol ang mga dokumentong kinakailangan para sa paglalabas ng red notice laban kay dating Congressman Zaldy Co. Kahit w...

item.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla's US Visit Highlights UK-US Relations
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla have commenced a state visit to Washington D.C., marking a significant moment in the ongoing relationship between the United Kingdom and the United States. The visit...

item.title: Coverage of the King's first day in the United States
item.description: Due to restricted access, the full content of the Sky News article about the King's first day in the United States could not be retrieved. However, the article likely summarised key moments from the i...

item.title: France Urges Iran to Make Significant Concessions Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: France's Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot has called on Iran to undertake substantial concessions to resolve ongoing tensions, particularly regarding its control of the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Sp...

item.title: The Tragic Deaths of Two Young Women at Mumbai’s Rajabai Tower in 1891
item.description: In 1891, two young Parsi women, Bachoobai and Phirojbai, fell to their deaths from the Rajabai Tower in Mumbai under mysterious circumstances. Their deaths, which occurred on April 25, sparked widespr...

item.title: Labour groups heavily funded Alberta’s petition to keep the province in Canada
item.description: Labour unions have emerged as the dominant third‑party advertisers backing Alberta’s anti‑separatist petition, outspending proponents of the rival pro‑independence campaign by a wide margin. The Unite...

item.title: Australia updates: Aukus submarine concerns, fuel reserve expansion, and ongoing domestic issues
item.description: Today's Australian news covers several key issues impacting national security, energy policy, and social matters. A UK parliamentary inquiry has identified significant shortcomings in the Aukus nuclea...

item.title: King Charles holds meeting with Trump to improve UK-US relations
item.description: Britain's King Charles has met with former US President Donald Trump in a diplomatic effort to strengthen and restore ties between the United Kingdom and the United States. The meeting, which took pla...

item.title: APA Reports 43% Profit Growth to Sh2.8 Billion, Driven by Insurance Success
item.description: APA Apollo Group has posted a notable 43% rise in net profit, reaching Sh2.8 billion for the year ending December 2025. This performance is attributed to increased insurance revenue and improved opera...

item.title: Three foreign nationals shot dead in Johannesburg McDonald's during apparent turf war attack
item.description: In a shocking incident in the Johannesburg CBD, three men aged between 30 and 45 were gunned down while enjoying breakfast at a McDonald's restaurant on the corner of Jeppe and Small streets. Two mask...

item.title: Senator Kingibe Condemns Death of NYSC Member, Calls for Investigation
item.description: Senator Ireti Kingibe don talk say she dey very, very pained by the death of Abdulsamad Jamiu, a young NYSC member wey allegedly die by hand of soldiers. The incident happen when soldiers dey do patro...

item.title: Trump Expresses Doubts on Iran's Hormuz Reopening Offer
item.description: In the context of ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, President Trump and his national security advisors have voiced significant skepticism toward Iran's latest proposal. The offer f...

item.title: Partly cloudy skies and light rain expected in Delhi after high temperatures
item.description: Delhi has been experiencing unusually high temperatures, with Safdarjung recording 42.3°C and the Ridge reaching 43.8°C on Monday, significantly above the normal averages. Although these levels were h...

item.title: Authorities distribute Rs 127.69 crore compensation to Rose Valley scam victims
item.description: In a significant step towards providing relief to victims of the Rose Valley chit fund scam, a probe agency has disbursed Rs 127.69 crore to around 1.73 lakh affected investors. The distribution is pa...

item.title: Proposal to Deploy NYS Graduates for Public Security to Save Government Money
item.description: A proposal in the National Assembly is stirring debate as it suggests replacing private security guards in public institutions with National Youth Service (NYS) graduates. Kilifi Woman Representative ...
